mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns during rate cuts are statistically indistinguishable from baseline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment remains below 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, which drives corporate revenue forecasts upward and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, or forward returns are negative in over 55% of co-occurring growth months over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices during post-shock recovery phases indicate rapid capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, which compresses input cost pressures and yields positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative forward returns during recovery phases, or its predictive accuracy drops below random chance when tested exclusively on post-2020 data.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 260.0, persistent inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 260.0, or the threshold shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses explanatory power in multivariate regression, or forward returns are negative in over 50% of months where both conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarterly GDP growth during late-cycle expansion phases reflects peak aggregate demand that maximizes corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: GDP growth signals produce negative forward returns during late-cycle phases, or the mechanism fails to predict returns when tested on historical recession-adjacent quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates combined with a rising unemployment rate indicate policy-induced economic deceleration, which suppresses earnings growth expectations and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint threshold condition fails to predict negative returns, or forward returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.
